canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Best-of-n sampling is consistently described as a procedure used at test-time or inference-time to select a single high-quality output from multiple candidates generated by a language model. The common mechanism involves an LLM generating N parallel solutions, which are then scored or ranked by a separate component, typically a verifier or a reward model, with the highest-scoring candidate being chosen. A prevalent use of this method is to evaluate the effectiveness of these verifiers and reward models; for instance, one study uses it to assess "the correctness of the most preferred trajectory selected by the PRM from n candidates" (Process Reward Model with Q-value Rankings). Other work notes it is a "common way to test the effectiveness of a reward design" (SelfCite: Self-Supervised Alignment for Context Attribution in Large Language Models). In addition to evaluation, some papers frame it as a direct method for performance enhancement, described as a "widely-used approach to improve the reasoning performance of LLMs" (Generative Verifiers: Reward Modeling as Next-Token Prediction). While the core process is stable, it is referred to by several names, including 'Best-of-N search' and 'Best-of-N selection', with one paper equating it to 'rejection sampling' (MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions). It is characterized as one of the "simplest and most conventional inference" strategies and is reported to achieve better performance than methods like PPO in some contexts.
expert_removed_reason: method/engineering — not a construct/measurement/behavior of LLM ability

definitions.text: An evaluation procedure where N candidate solutions are generated by an LLM, and a verifier is used to rank them and select the best one.
definitions.paper: Generative Verifiers: Reward Modeling as Next-Token Prediction

definitions.text: A verification evaluation procedure in which a process reward model scores multiple candidate trajectories for each question and the most preferred trajectory is selected from n candidates.
definitions.paper: Process Reward Model with Q-value Rankings

definitions.text: A rejection-sampling procedure that selects the highest-reward response from multiple sampled candidates.
definitions.paper: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions

definitions.text: A planning and evaluation procedure where an agent generates N independent trajectories, and a reward model selects the one with the highest score.
definitions.paper: Scaling Autonomous Agents via Automatic Reward Modeling And Planning

definitions.text: A test-time inference procedure that samples multiple candidate solutions in parallel and selects the one judged most likely to be correct.
definitions.paper: Token-Supervised Value Models for Enhancing Mathematical Problem-Solving Capabilities of Large Language Models

definitions.text: A test-time response selection procedure that scores multiple candidate responses and chooses the highest-scoring one.
definitions.paper: The Devil Is in the Details: Tackling Unimodal Spurious Correlations for Generalizable Multimodal Reward Models

definitions.text: An evaluation method where N candidate responses are generated from a model, and a reward model is used to select the best one, with the final performance judged by a separate 'golden' reward model.
definitions.paper: Rethinking Reward Modeling in Preference-based Large Language Model Alignment

characterization: Across the provided literature, Best-of-n sampling is consistently described as a procedure used at test-time or inference-time to select a single high-quality output from multiple candidates generated by a language model. The common mechanism involves an LLM generating N parallel solutions, which are then scored or ranked by a separate component, typically a verifier or a reward model, with the highest-scoring candidate being chosen. A prevalent use of this method is to evaluate the effectiveness of these verifiers and reward models; for instance, one study uses it to assess "the correctness of the most preferred trajectory selected by the PRM from n candidates" (Process Reward Model with Q-value Rankings). Other work notes it is a "common way to test the effectiveness of a reward design" (SelfCite: Self-Supervised Alignment for Context Attribution in Large Language Models). In addition to evaluation, some papers frame it as a direct method for performance enhancement, described as a "widely-used approach to improve the reasoning performance of LLMs" (Generative Verifiers: Reward Modeling as Next-Token Prediction). While the core process is stable, it is referred to by several names, including 'Best-of-N search' and 'Best-of-N selection', with one paper equating it to 'rejection sampling' (MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions). It is characterized as one of the "simplest and most conventional inference" strategies and is reported to achieve better performance than methods like PPO in some contexts.
